title: Cessna-maker Textron plans to cut 725 positions, or 2% of workforce
text: Textron Inc. TXT, the company that makes Cessna airplanes and other aircraft, said on Tuesday that it would cut around 725 positions, or 2% of its global staff, following reduced product demand for some of its businesses. The company, in a filing, cited “lower demand for certain of our powersports products which we anticipate will continue,” along with “reduced demand for fuel systems from European automotive manufacturers.

title: Textron to cut 725 jobs as part of restructuring plan
text: Defense manufacturer Textron said on Tuesday it would cut 725 jobs as part of its restructuring plan in an attempt to reduce operating expenses.
